cases.question: Walk me through how you would investigate a failing NVIDIA/Megatron-LM GitHub Actions job URL and file a useful bug for it.
cases.ground_truth: Parse the run and job IDs, inspect the job logs, identify the failed pytest node and concise root error, resolve the triggering PR and test-file author, search for duplicates, and only then create a linked and assigned bug issue.
expected_behavior: Extracts the run and job identifiers and inspects the actual failure logs
Resolves the triggering PR and the failing test file's most recent author
Searches open issues for a duplicate before creating anything
Uses a concise issue body with PR, run, job, failed test, and no more than 30 lines of error output

cases.question: This GitHub Actions run URL has three failed jobs. Create an issue for the failure.
cases.ground_truth: A run URL must first be expanded into its failed jobs. When several jobs failed, ask which job to triage unless the user explicitly asks to triage all of them.
expected_behavior: Lists the failed jobs from the run
Asks the user which failed job to triage or offers to triage all
Does not guess which failure the user intended

cases.question: The failing test is tests/unit_tests/models/test_gpt_model.py::TestGPTModel::test_constructor, and an open issue already tracks that exact failure. Please file another bug from the CI run.
cases.ground_truth: Do not create a duplicate. Return the existing issue to the user and stop.
expected_behavior: Recognizes the matching open issue as a duplicate
Does not create a new issue

cases.question: Why is this Megatron-LM CI pipeline red, and which check should I rerun? Do not create an issue.
cases.ground_truth: CI diagnosis without an issue-creation request belongs to mcore-cicd, not mcore-create-issue.
expected_behavior: Routes the diagnosis request to mcore-cicd
Does not create or propose creating a GitHub issue
